attributes.action: Make organ donation consent made prior to someone’s death binding
attributes.additional_details: Under the current system, families are still consulted and can effectively prevent donation, even when a person has clearly stated their wishes. We think this undermines personal autonomy and can result in fewer lifesaving transplants.
attributes.background: We call on the Government to legislate so that a person’s registered decision to donate is legally binding and cannot be overridden by family members after the person’s death.

departments.name: Department of Health and Social Care
